title: Align Hachi dripper tests with engine TP-level gating

root_cause.summary: load_dotenv(override=True) in config.py overwrites monkeypatch.setenv values, making test fixture settings ineffective.
detail: Tests in test_hachi_brain.py call monkeypatch.setenv('TAKE_PROFIT_LEVELS_PCT', '0,25,60,120,250') to set a 0% first TP level so fresh positions are eligible for drips.
load_settings() then calls load_dotenv(env_path, override=True), which reads .env (TAKE_PROFIT_LEVELS_PCT=25,60,120,250) and overwrites the monkeypatch value.

After stomping, tp_levels = [25.0, 60.0, 120.0, 250.0]. For pnl_pct=10.0, current_tp_level stays at -1 (no level reached, since 10 < 25). The TP gate sees current_tp_level < 0 → eligible_tp = False → emits DRIPPER_WAIT.
Tests correctly expected DRIPPER_CHUNK_SELECTED. The engine TP-gate logic is correct. The problem is configuration loading order.
not_affected: G-3 (test_deterioration_weakening_uses_aggressive_chunk): already expects DRIPPER_WAIT — passes regardless.
G-4 / G-4b: URGENCY_OVERRIDE_FULL bypasses TP gate entirely — passes regardless.
G-6 / G-7 / G-10 / G-11: use _evaluate_exit_rules path or test timing gate independently — not sensitive to TP levels value.

engine_verdict.explanation: The TP-gate logic in _run_hachi_dripper is correct and intentional: gate drips to once per TP level, preventing repeated liquidation of a runner at the same price band. The engine emitting DRIPPER_WAIT when no TP level has been crossed is the expected production behaviour.

tests_verdict.explanation: The tests correctly model the intended behaviour. Commit 779be4a updated the fixture to set TAKE_PROFIT_LEVELS_PCT=0,25,60,120,250 specifically to make pnl_pct < 25% eligible via the 0% sentinel level. The intent was correct; the mechanism (monkeypatch.setenv) was silently defeated by load_dotenv(override=True) loading .env after monkeypatch had already set the env var.

rationale: override=False means pre-existing os.environ values (set by monkeypatch.setenv in tests) take precedence over .env file values.
In production, no env vars are pre-set before load_settings() is called, so override=False vs True makes no functional difference to live trading.
In tests, monkeypatch.setenv values now correctly win over .env, restoring test fixture isolation.
fix_applied.production_impact: None. Production runs with no pre-existing env vars set, so .env is still the sole source of truth for all settings.
fix_applied.trading_behaviour_impact: None. No trading logic changed. Only test isolation restored.
fix_applied.cu_impact: None. No Birdeye or API call paths touched.

validation_for_cursor.full_suite_regression_check: .venv/bin/python -m pytest --tb=short -q (expect 207 passed, 0 or 1 failed — only test_setup_logging_creates_per_run_logfile may remain as a known pre-existing failure if T-001 is not yet applied)

commit_message:
fix: restore test fixture isolation in load_settings() (T-002)

Changes load_dotenv(override=True) to override=False so that env vars
already set in os.environ (e.g. via pytest monkeypatch) are not
overwritten by .env file values. Production behaviour is unchanged:
no env vars are pre-set before load_settings() in a live run, so .env
remains the sole source of truth. Fixes G-1/G-2/G-5/G-8/G-9 failures
in test_hachi_brain.py.
